assertion_results.text: The review comment about transaction wrapping (id 30001) is classified as a code change request and triggers sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30001.md states '## Classification: code change request' and 'A sub-task will be created.' The file subtask-30001.md exists with the corresponding sub-task description. report.md confirms: '30001 | ... | Code change request | Sub-task created (wrap updates in transaction)'.

assertion_results.text: The review comment about adding an index (id 30002) is classified as suggestion — the reviewer uses suggestive language ('should also', 'would help') and the classification output (review-30002.md) explains this reasoning
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md states '## Classification: suggestion' and reasoning includes: '"should also add" -- the word "also" signals this is an addition beyond the core requirements' and '"would help" -- conditional/hedging language indicating the reviewer is proposing an optimization rather than requiring a fix' and '"Something like:" -- the reviewer provides a code example prefaced by "something like", indicating this is one possible approach rather than a prescribed fix.'

assertion_results.text: The nit comment about context message (id 30003) is classified as nit and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30003.md states '## Classification: nit' and 'Classified as **nit**. No sub-task created.' No subtask-30003.md file exists in the outputs directory.

assertion_results.text: The question comment about GET behavior (id 30004) is classified as question and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30004.md states '## Classification: question' and 'Classified as **question**. No sub-task created.' No subtask-30004.md file exists in the outputs directory.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description follows the task-description-template.md format: contains Repository, Description, Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, and Acceptance Criteria sections (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md (the only sub-task) contains all required sections: '## Repository' (line 1), '## Description' (line 7), '## Files to Modify' (line 10), '## Implementation Notes' (line 13), '## Acceptance Criteria' (line 20).

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Target PR section with the PR URL https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744 (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains '## Target PR' (line 32) followed by 'https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744' (line 33).

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Review Context section with the original review comment text (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains '## Review Context' (line 35) with Comment ID 30001, Author reviewer-a, File and line reference, and the full original comment text: 'The `soft_delete` method should run all three UPDATE statements inside a single database transaction...'

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only classification files (review-30001.md through review-30004.md), one sub-task description (subtask-30001.md), and the report (report.md). No code source files (.rs, .toml, etc.) appear in the outputs. The report does not indicate any code files were modified.

assertion_results.text: The skill reads and processes all PR review comments before generating findings (constraint 1.10)
assertion_results.evidence: All four review comments are processed: review-30001.md, review-30002.md, review-30003.md, review-30004.md all exist with full classifications. The report's Review Comment Summary table lists all 4 comments (30001, 30002, 30003, 30004) with their classifications and actions.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: The report's overall result is 'FAIL' and there is no mention of merging, auto-merging, or approving the PR anywhere in report.md. The report concludes with analysis details only.

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md contains '| Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | 1 new test file (tests/api/sbom_delete.rs) with 5 test functions; no modified or deleted test files |' and the Test Change Classification Details section states 'Classification: **ADDITIVE**'.

assertion_results.text: Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion) — the review classification output (review-30002.md) or the report's Style/Conventions analysis explains whether the suggestion matches a documented or demonstrated project convention
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md contains a '### Convention Upgrade Eligibility' section that checks CONVENTIONS.md ('no content is available in the fixture data') and codebase patterns ('The PR diff does not contain other migration files to count index creation patterns'). It concludes: 'Without convention data backing an upgrade, this suggestion remains classified as **suggestion**. No sub-task created.'

assertion_results.text: Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) does NOT result in a sub-task — the suggestion classification is correct (suggestive language, no directive) and no project convention in the fixture data backs an upgrade from suggestion to code change request
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md states 'No sub-task created.' No subtask-30002.md file exists in the outputs directory. report.md confirms: '30002 | ... | Suggestion | No sub-task (no convention data to support upgrade)'.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — the 3-criteria detection (author github-actions[bot], marker ## Eval Results, footer sdlc-workflow/run-evals) found no matches, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md Test Quality row states 'Eval Quality: N/A' and the Test Quality Details section states 'Eval Quality: N/A -- no eval result reviews found on the PR.'

assertion_results.text: The sub-task creation for comment 30001 explicitly specifies Issue Type as Sub-task — the subtask-30001.md file, the report's sub-task section, or the review classification output (review-30001.md) indicates the Jira issue is created with issueTypeName Sub-task (not as a standalone Task) to ensure parent-child hierarchy with the parent task
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains '## Issue Type' (line 29) followed by 'Sub-task' (line 30), explicitly specifying the Jira issue type as Sub-task.
